key_facts: Foreign-run solar fintech operating inside China's distributed solar market
Connects multinational factory-owners in China with solar project investors via PPA
Delivers 10–15% electricity savings for industrial building customers
Described as 'analogous to early SolarCity but focused on industrial buildings'
Model: C&I rooftop PPA for foreign-owned factories

funding_history.notes: Small seed; limited public information on subsequent rounds or revenue

business_model_notes: Niche C&I solar PPA focusing on foreign-owned factories in China. Connects investors (ROI-seeking capital) with factory-owner customers (electricity cost savings). Very small scale ($438K seed) vs Asia Clean Capital which secured EDF institutional capital. Likely operates as intermediary/broker rather than balance-sheet asset owner.
competitive_position: Extremely niche. Low public profile. Appears to have remained small relative to Asia Clean Capital and domestic PPA operators. The model requires access to both corporate treasury decision-makers (for factory PPAs) and institutional investors (for project capital) — a narrow Venn diagram.
research_notes: LOW CONFIDENCE. Minimal English-language press coverage. Crunchbase entry confirms existence and seed funding. energystartups.org profile provides model description. No subsequent funding rounds found; may have remained boutique or shut down. Status as of 2026 unclear.
